NKJV.1: After these things I saw four angels standing at the four corners of the earth, holding the four winds of the earth, that the wind should not blow on the earth, on the sea, or on any tree.
NKJV.2: Then I saw another angel ascending from the east, having the seal of the living God. And he cried with a loud voice to the four angels to whom it was granted to harm the earth and the sea,
NKJV.3: saying, “Do not harm the earth, the sea, or the trees till we have sealed the servants of our God on their foreheads.”
NKJV.4: And I heard the number of those who were sealed. One hundred and forty-four thousand of all the tribes of the children of Israel were sealed:
NKJV.5: of the tribe of Judah twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Reuben twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Gad twelve thousand were sealed;
NKJV.6: of the tribe of Asher twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Naphtali twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Manasseh twelve thousand were sealed;
NKJV.7: of the tribe of Simeon twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Levi twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Issachar twelve thousand were sealed;
NKJV.8: of the tribe of Zebulun twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Joseph twelve thousand were sealed; of the tribe of Benjamin twelve thousand were sealed.
NKJV.9: After these things I looked, and behold, a great multitude which no one could number, of all nations, tribes, peoples, and tongues, standing before the throne and before the Lamb, clothed with white robes, with palm branches in their hands,
NKJV.10: and crying out with a loud voice, saying, “Salvation belongs to our God who sits on the throne, and to the Lamb!”
NKJV.11: All the angels stood around the throne and the elders and the four living creatures, and fell on their faces before the throne and worshiped God,
NKJV.12: saying: “Amen! Blessing and glory and wisdom, Thanksgiving and honor and power and might, Be to our God forever and ever. Amen.”
NKJV.13: Then one of the elders answered, saying to me, “Who are these arrayed in white robes, and where did they come from?”
NKJV.14: And I said to him, “Sir, you know.” So he said to me, “These are the ones who come out of the great tribulation, and washed their robes and made them white in the blood of the Lamb.
NKJV.15: Therefore they are before the throne of God, and serve Him day and night in His temple. And He who sits on the throne will dwell among them.
NKJV.16: They shall neither hunger anymore nor thirst anymore; the sun shall not strike them, nor any heat;
NKJV.17: for the Lamb who is in the midst of the throne will shepherd them and lead them to living fountains of waters. And God will wipe away every tear from their eyes.”

ESV.1: After this I saw four angels standing at the four corners of the earth, holding back the four winds of the earth, that no wind might blow on earth or sea or against any tree.
ESV.2: Then I saw another angel ascending from the rising of the sun, with the seal of the living God, and he called with a loud voice to the four angels who had been given power to harm earth and sea,
ESV.3: saying, “Do not harm the earth or the sea or the trees, until we have sealed the servants of our God on their foreheads.”
ESV.4: And I heard the number of the sealed, 144,000, sealed from every tribe of the sons of Israel:
ESV.5: 12,000 from the tribe of Judah were sealed, 12,000 from the tribe of Reuben, 12,000 from the tribe of Gad,
ESV.6: 12,000 from the tribe of Asher, 12,000 from the tribe of Naphtali, 12,000 from the tribe of Manasseh,
ESV.7: 12,000 from the tribe of Simeon, 12,000 from the tribe of Levi, 12,000 from the tribe of Issachar,
ESV.8: 12,000 from the tribe of Zebulun, 12,000 from the tribe of Joseph, 12,000 from the tribe of Benjamin were sealed.
ESV.9: After this I looked, and behold, a great multitude that no one could number, from every nation, from all tribes and peoples and languages, standing before the throne and before the Lamb, clothed in white robes, with palm branches in their hands,
ESV.10: and crying out with a loud voice, “Salvation belongs to our God who sits on the throne, and to the Lamb!”
ESV.11: And all the angels were standing around the throne and around the elders and the four living creatures, and they fell on their faces before the throne and worshiped God,
ESV.12: saying, “Amen! Blessing and glory and wisdom and thanksgiving and honor and power and might be to our God forever and ever! Amen.”
ESV.13: Then one of the elders addressed me, saying, “Who are these, clothed in white robes, and from where have they come?”
ESV.14: I said to him, “Sir, you know.” And he said to me, “These are the ones coming out of the great tribulation. They have washed their robes and made them white in the blood of the Lamb.
ESV.15: “Therefore they are before the throne of God, and serve him day and night in his temple; and he who sits on the throne will shelter them with his presence.
ESV.16: They shall hunger no more, neither thirst anymore; the sun shall not strike them, nor any scorching heat.
ESV.17: For the Lamb in the midst of the throne will be their shepherd, and he will guide them to springs of living water, and God will wipe away every tear from their eyes.”

NASB.1: After this I saw four angels standing at the four corners of the earth, holding back the four winds of the earth, so that no wind would blow on the earth or on the sea or on any tree.
NASB.2: And I saw another angel ascending from the rising of the sun, having the seal of the living God; and he cried out with a loud voice to the four angels to whom it was granted to harm the earth and the sea,
NASB.3: saying, 'Do not harm the earth or the sea or the trees until we have sealed the bond-servants of our God on their foreheads.'
NASB.4: And I heard the number of those who were sealed, one hundred and forty-four thousand sealed from every tribe of the sons of Israel:
NASB.5: from the tribe of Judah, twelve thousand [were] sealed, from the tribe of Reuben twelve thousand, from the tribe of Gad twelve thousand,
NASB.6: from the tribe of Asher twelve thousand, from the tribe of Naphtali twelve thousand, from the tribe of Manasseh twelve thousand,
NASB.7: from the tribe of Simeon twelve thousand, from the tribe of Levi twelve thousand, from the tribe of Issachar twelve thousand,
NASB.8: from the tribe of Zebulun twelve thousand, from the tribe of Joseph twelve thousand, from the tribe of Benjamin, twelve thousand [were] sealed.
NASB.9: After these things I looked, and behold, a great multitude which no one could count, from every nation and [all] tribes and peoples and tongues, standing before the throne and before the Lamb, clothed in white robes, and palm branches [were] in their hands;
NASB.10: and they cry out with a loud voice, saying, 'Salvation to our God who sits on the throne, and to the Lamb.'
NASB.11: And all the angels were standing around the throne and [around] the elders and the four living creatures; and they fell on their faces before the throne and worshiped God,
NASB.12: saying, 'Amen, blessing and glory and wisdom and thanksgiving and honor and power and might, [be] to our God forever and ever. Amen.'
NASB.13: Then one of the elders answered, saying to me, 'These who are clothed in the white robes, who are they, and where have they come from?'
NASB.14: I said to him, 'My lord, you know.' And he said to me, 'These are the ones who come out of the great tribulation, and they have washed their robes and made them white in the blood of the Lamb.
NASB.15: 'For this reason, they are before the throne of God; and they serve Him day and night in His temple; and He who sits on the throne will spread His tabernacle over them.
NASB.16: 'They will hunger no longer, nor thirst anymore; nor will the sun beat down on them, nor any heat;
NASB.17: for the Lamb in the center of the throne will be their shepherd, and will guide them to springs of the water of life; and God will wipe every tear from their eyes.'

NLT.1: Then I saw four angels standing at the four corners of the earth, holding back the four winds so they did not blow on the earth or the sea, or even on any tree.
NLT.2: And I saw another angel coming up from the east, carrying the seal of the living God. And he shouted to those four angels, who had been given power to harm land and sea,
NLT.3: “Wait! Don’t harm the land or the sea or the trees until we have placed the seal of God on the foreheads of his servants.”
NLT.4: And I heard how many were marked with the seal of God — 144,000 were sealed from all the tribes of Israel:
NLT.9: After this I saw a vast crowd, too great to count, from every nation and tribe and people and language, standing in front of the throne and before the Lamb. They were clothed in white robes and held palm branches in their hands.
NLT.10:
And they were shouting with a great roar, “Salvation comes from our God who sits on the throne
and from the Lamb!”
NLT.11: And all the angels were standing around the throne and around the elders and the four living beings. And they fell before the throne with their faces to the ground and worshiped God.
NLT.12:
They sang, “Amen! Blessing and glory and wisdom
and thanksgiving and honor
and power and strength belong to our God
forever and ever! Amen.”
NLT.13: Then one of the twenty-four elders asked me, “Who are these who are clothed in white? Where did they come from?”
NLT.14: And I said to him, “Sir, you are the one who knows.” Then he said to me, “These are the ones who died in the great tribulation. They have washed their robes in the blood of the Lamb and made them white.
NLT.15:
“That is why they stand in front of God’s throne
and serve him day and night in his Temple.
And he who sits on the throne
will give them shelter.
NLT.16:
They will never again be hungry or thirsty;
they will never be scorched by the heat of the sun.
NLT.17:
For the Lamb on the throne
will be their Shepherd.
He will lead them to springs of life-giving water.
And God will wipe every tear from their eyes.”

WEB.1: After this, I saw four angels standing at the four corners of the earth, holding the four winds of the earth, so that no wind would blow on the earth, or on the sea, or on any tree.
WEB.2: I saw another angel ascend from the sunrise, having the seal of the living God. He cried with a loud voice to the four angels to whom it was given to harm the earth and the sea,
WEB.3: saying, “Don’t harm the earth, neither the sea, nor the trees, until we have sealed the bondservants of our God on their foreheads!”
WEB.4: I heard the number of those who were sealed, one hundred forty-four thousand, sealed out of every tribe of the children of Israel:
WEB.5: of the tribe of Judah were sealed twelve thousand, of the tribe of Reuben twelve thousand, of the tribe of Gad twelve thousand,
WEB.6: of the tribe of Asher twelve thousand, of the tribe of Naphtali twelve thousand, of the tribe of Manasseh twelve thousand,
WEB.7: of the tribe of Simeon twelve thousand, of the tribe of Levi twelve thousand, of the tribe of Issachar twelve thousand,
WEB.8: of the tribe of Zebulun twelve thousand, of the tribe of Joseph twelve thousand, of the tribe of Benjamin were sealed twelve thousand.
WEB.9: After these things I looked, and behold, a great multitude, which no man could count, out of every nation and of all tribes, peoples, and languages, standing before the throne and before the Lamb, dressed in white robes, with palm branches in their hands.
WEB.10: They cried with a loud voice, saying, “Salvation be to our God, who sits on the throne, and to the Lamb!”
WEB.11: All the angels were standing around the throne, the elders, and the four living creatures; and they fell on their faces before his throne, and worshiped God,
WEB.12: saying, “Amen! Blessing, glory, wisdom, thanksgiving, honor, power, and might, be to our God forever and ever! Amen.”
WEB.13: One of the elders answered, saying to me, “These who are arrayed in the white robes, who are they, and from where did they come?”
WEB.14: I told him, “My lord, you know.” He said to me, “These are those who came out of the great tribulation. They washed their robes, and made them white in the Lamb’s blood.
WEB.15: Therefore they are before the throne of God, they serve him day and night in his temple. He who sits on the throne will spread his tabernacle over them.
WEB.16: They will never be hungry, neither thirsty any more; neither will the sun beat on them, nor any heat;
WEB.17: for the Lamb who is in the middle of the throne shepherds them, and leads them to springs of waters of life. And God will wipe away every tear from their eyes.”

CSB17.1: After this I saw four angels <sup>ⓗ</sup> standing at the four corners of the earth, restraining the four winds of the earth <sup>ⓘ</sup> so that no wind could blow on the earth or on the sea or on any tree.
CSB17.2: Then I saw another angel rising up from the east, <sup>ⓙ</sup> who had the seal of the living God. <sup>ⓚ</sup> He cried out in a loud voice to the four angels who were allowed to harm the earth and the sea:
CSB17.3: “Don’t harm the earth or the sea or the trees <sup>ⓛ</sup> until we seal the servants of our God on their foreheads.” <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.4: And I heard the number of the sealed: 144,000 sealed from every tribe of the Israelites: ⓝ
CSB17.5: 12,000 sealed from the tribe of Judah, 12,000 [1] from the tribe of Reuben, 12,000 from the tribe of Gad,
CSB17.6: 12,000 from the tribe of Asher, 12,000 from the tribe of Naphtali, 12,000 from the tribe of Manasseh,
CSB17.7: 12,000 from the tribe of Simeon, 12,000 from the tribe of Levi, 12,000 from the tribe of Issachar,
CSB17.8: 12,000 from the tribe of Zebulun, 12,000 from the tribe of Joseph, 12,000 sealed from the tribe of Benjamin.
CSB17.9: After this I looked, and there was a vast multitude from every nation, tribe, people, and language, <sup>ⓐ</sup> which no one could number, <sup>ⓑ</sup> standing before the throne and before the Lamb. They were clothed in white robes with palm branches in their hands. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.10:
And they cried out in a loud voice: Salvation belongs to our God, ⓓ
who is seated on the throne,
and to the Lamb!
CSB17.11: All the angels stood around the throne, and along with the elders and the four living creatures they fell facedown before the throne and worshiped God,
CSB17.12:
saying, Amen! Blessing and glory and wisdom
and thanksgiving and honor
and power and strength
be to our God forever and ever. ⓔ Amen.
CSB17.13: Then one of the elders asked me, “Who are these people in white robes, and where did they come from?”
CSB17.14: I said to him, “Sir, <sup>[2]</sup> you know.” Then he told me: These are the ones coming out of the great tribulation. <sup>ⓕ</sup> They washed their robes and made them white <sup>ⓖ</sup> in the blood of the Lamb.
CSB17.15:
For this reason they are before the throne of God,
and they serve him day and night ⓗ in his temple. ⓘ
The one seated on the throne will shelter [3] them: ⓙ
CSB17.16:
They will no longer hunger;
they will no longer thirst;
the sun will no longer strike them,
nor will any scorching heat. ⓚ
CSB17.17:
For the Lamb who is at the center of the throne
will shepherd them; ⓛ
he will guide them to springs of the waters of life, ⓜ
and God will wipe away every tear from their eyes. ⓝ

AMP.1: After this I saw four angels stationed at the four corners of the earth, holding back the four winds of the earth so that no wind would blow on the earth or on the sea or on any tree.
AMP.2: Then I saw another angel coming up from the rising of the sun, holding the seal of the living God; and with a loud voice he called out to the four angels to whom it was granted [to have authority and power] to harm the earth and the sea,
AMP.3: saying, "Do not harm the earth nor the sea nor the trees until we seal (mark) the bond-servants of our God on their foreheads."
AMP.4: And I heard how many were sealed, a hundred and forty-four thousand; [twelve thousand] sealed from every tribe of the sons of Israel:
AMP.5: Twelve thousand were sealed from the tribe of Judah, twelve thousand from the tribe of Reuben, twelve thousand from the tribe of Gad,
AMP.6: twelve thousand from the tribe of Asher, twelve thousand from the tribe of Naphtali, twelve thousand from the tribe of Manasseh,
AMP.7: twelve thousand from the tribe of Simeon, twelve thousand from the tribe of Levi, twelve thousand from the tribe of Issachar,
AMP.8: twelve thousand from the tribe of Zebulun, twelve thousand from the tribe of Joseph, and twelve thousand from the tribe of Benjamin were sealed (marked, redeemed, protected).
AMP.9: After these things I looked, and this is what I saw: a vast multitude which no one could count, [gathered] from every nation and from all the tribes and peoples and languages [of the earth], standing before the throne and before the Lamb (Christ), dressed in white robes, with palm branches in their hands;
AMP.10:
and in a loud voice they cried out, saying,
"Salvation [belongs] to our God who is seated on the throne, and to the Lamb [our salvation is the Trinity's to give, and to God the Trinity we owe our deliverance]."
AMP.11: And all the angels were standing around the throne and around the [twenty-four] elders and the four living creatures; and they fell to their faces before the throne and worshiped God,
AMP.12:
saying,
"Amen! Blessing and glory and majesty and wisdom and thanksgiving and honor and power and might belong to our God forever and ever. Amen."
AMP.13: Then one of the elders responded, saying to me, "These who are dressed in the long white robes—who are they, and from where did they come?"
AMP.14: I said to him, "My lord, you know [the answer]." And he said to me, "These are the people who come out of the great tribulation (persecution), and they have washed their robes and made them white in the blood of the Lamb [because of His atoning sacrifice].
AMP.15: For this reason, they are [standing] before the throne of God; and they serve Him [in worship] day and night in His temple; and He who sits on the throne will spread His tabernacle over them and shelter and protect them [with His presence].
AMP.16: They will hunger no longer, nor thirst anymore; nor will the sun beat down on them, nor any [scorching] heat;
AMP.17: for the Lamb who is in the center of the throne will be their Shepherd, and He will guide them to springs of the waters of life; and God will wipe every tear from their eyes [giving them eternal comfort]."

MSG.1: Immediately I saw Four Angels standing at the four corners of earth, standing steady with a firm grip on the four winds so no wind would blow on earth or sea, not even rustle a tree.
MSG.2: Then I saw another Angel rising from where the sun rose, carrying the seal of the Living God. He thundered to the Four Angels assigned the task of hurting earth and sea,
MSG.3: "Don't hurt the earth! Don't hurt the sea! Don't so much as hurt a tree until I've sealed the servants of our God on their foreheads!"
MSG.4: I heard the count of those who were sealed: 144,000! They were sealed out of every Tribe of Israel:
MSG.5: 12,000 sealed from Judah, 12,000 from Reuben, 12,000 from Gad,
MSG.6: 12,000 from Asher, 12,000 from Naphtali, 12,000 from Manasseh,
MSG.7: 12,000 from Simeon, 12,000 from Levi, 12,000 from Issachar,
MSG.8: 12,000 from Zebulun, 12,000 from Joseph, 12,000 sealed from Benjamin.
MSG.9: I looked again. I saw a huge crowd, too huge to count. Everyone was there—all nations and tribes, all races and languages. And they were standing, dressed in white robes and waving palm branches, standing before the Throne and the Lamb
MSG.10: and heartily singing: Salvation to our God on his Throne! Salvation to the Lamb!
MSG.11: All who were standing around the Throne—Angels, Elders, Animals—fell on their faces before the Throne and worshiped God,
MSG.12: singing: Oh, Yes! The blessing and glory and wisdom and thanksgiving, The honor and power and strength, To our God forever and ever and ever! Oh, Yes!
MSG.13: Just then one of the Elders addressed me: "Who are these dressed in white robes, and where did they come from?"
MSG.14: Taken aback, I said, "O Sir, I have no idea—but you must know." Then he told me, "These are those who come from the great tribulation, and they've washed their robes, scrubbed them clean in the blood of the Lamb.
MSG.15: That's why they're standing before God's Throne. They serve him day and night in his Temple. The One on the Throne will pitch his tent there for them:
MSG.16: no more hunger, no more thirst, no more scorching heat.
MSG.17: The Lamb on the Throne will shepherd them, will lead them to spring waters of Life. And God will wipe every last tear from their eyes."

NIV.1:
144,000 Sealed
After this I saw four angels standing at the four corners of the earth, holding back the four winds of the earth to prevent any wind from blowing on the land or on the sea or on any tree.
NIV.2: Then I saw another angel coming up from the east, having the seal of the living God. He called out in a loud voice to the four angels who had been given power to harm the land and the sea:
NIV.3: “Do not harm the land or the sea or the trees until we put a seal on the foreheads of the servants of our God.”
NIV.4: Then I heard the number of those who were sealed: 144,000 from all the tribes of Israel.
NIV.5:
From the tribe of Judah 12,000 were sealed,
from the tribe of Reuben 12,000,
from the tribe of Gad 12,0,
NIV.6:
from the tribe of Asher 12,000,
from the tribe of Naphtali 12,000,
from the tribe of Manasseh 12,0,
NIV.7:
from the tribe of Simeon 12,000,
from the tribe of Levi 12,000,
from the tribe of Issachar 12,0,
NIV.8:
from the tribe of Zebulun 12,000,
from the tribe of Joseph 12,000,
from the tribe of Benjamin 12,000.
NIV.9:
The Great Multitude in White Robes
After this I looked and there before me was a great multitude that no one could count, from every nation, tribe, people and language, standing before the throne and in front of the Lamb. They were wearing white robes and were holding palm branches in their hands.
NIV.10:
And they cried out in a loud voice:
“Salvation belongs to our God,
who sits on the throne,
and to the Lamb.”
NIV.11: All the angels were standing around the throne and around the elders and the four living creatures. They fell down on their faces before the throne and worshiped God,
NIV.12:
saying:
“Amen!
Praise and glory
and wisdom and thanks and honor
and power and strength
be to our God for ever and ever.
Amen!”
NIV.13: Then one of the elders asked me, “These in white robes—who are they, and where did they come from?”
NIV.14:
I answered, “Sir, you know.”
And he said, “These are they who have come out of the great tribulation; they have washed their robes and made them white in the blood of the Lamb.
NIV.15:
Therefore,
“they are before the throne of God
and serve him day and night in his temple;
and he who sits on the throne will spread his tent over them.
NIV.16:
Never again will they hunger;
never again will they thirst.
The sun will not beat upon them,
nor any scorching heat.
NIV.17:
For the Lamb at the center of the throne will be their shepherd;
he will lead them to springs of living water.
And God will wipe away every tear from their eyes.”

NRSVCE.1: After this I saw four angels standing at the four corners of the earth, holding back the four winds of the earth so that no wind could blow on earth or sea or against any tree.
NRSVCE.2: I saw another angel ascending from the rising of the sun, having the seal of the living God, and he called with a loud voice to the four angels who had been given power to damage earth and sea,
NRSVCE.3: saying, “Do not damage the earth or the sea or the trees, until we have marked the servants of our God with a seal on their foreheads.”
NRSVCE.4: And I heard the number of those who were sealed, one hundred forty-four thousand, sealed out of every tribe of the people of Israel:
NRSVCE.5:
From the tribe of Judah twelve thousand sealed,
from the tribe of Reuben twelve thousand,
from the tribe of Gad twelve thousand,
NRSVCE.6:
from the tribe of Asher twelve thousand,
from the tribe of Naphtali twelve thousand,
from the tribe of Manasseh twelve thousand,
NRSVCE.7:
from the tribe of Simeon twelve thousand,
from the tribe of Levi twelve thousand,
from the tribe of Issachar twelve thousand,
NRSVCE.8:
from the tribe of Zebulun twelve thousand,
from the tribe of Joseph twelve thousand,
from the tribe of Benjamin twelve thousand sealed.
NRSVCE.9: After this I looked, and there was a great multitude that no one could count, from every nation, from all tribes and peoples and languages, standing before the throne and before the Lamb, robed in white, with palm branches in their hands.
NRSVCE.10: They cried out in a loud voice, saying, “Salvation belongs to our God who is seated on the throne, and to the Lamb!”
NRSVCE.11: And all the angels stood around the throne and around the elders and the four living creatures, and they fell on their faces before the throne and worshiped God,
NRSVCE.12:
singing, “Amen! Blessing and glory and wisdom
and thanksgiving and honor
and power and might
be to our God forever and ever! Amen.”
NRSVCE.13: Then one of the elders addressed me, saying, “Who are these, robed in white, and where have they come from?”
NRSVCE.14: I said to him, “Sir, you are the one that knows.” Then he said to me, “These are they who have come out of the great ordeal; they have washed their robes and made them white in the blood of the Lamb.
NRSVCE.15:
For this reason they are before the throne of God,
and worship him day and night within his temple,
and the one who is seated on the throne will shelter them.
NRSVCE.16:
They will hunger no more, and thirst no more;
the sun will not strike them,
nor any scorching heat;
NRSVCE.17:
for the Lamb at the center of the throne will be their shepherd,
and he will guide them to springs of the water of life,
and God will wipe away every tear from their eyes.”

NET.1: After this I saw four angels standing at the four corners of the earth, holding back the four winds of the earth so no wind could blow on the earth, on the sea, or on any tree.
NET.2: Then I saw another angel ascending from the east, who had the seal of the living God. He shouted out with a loud voice to the four angels who had been given permission to damage the earth and the sea:
NET.3: “Do not damage the earth or the sea or the trees until we have put a seal on the foreheads of the servants of our God.”
NET.4: Now I heard the number of those who were marked with the seal, one hundred and forty-four thousand, sealed from all the tribes of the people of Israel:
NET.5: From the tribe of Judah, twelve thousand were sealed, from the tribe of Reuben, twelve thousand, from the tribe of Gad, twelve thousand,
NET.6: from the tribe of Asher, twelve thousand, from the tribe of Naphtali, twelve thousand, from the tribe of Manasseh, twelve thousand,
NET.7: from the tribe of Simeon, twelve thousand, from the tribe of Levi, twelve thousand, from the tribe of Issachar, twelve thousand,
NET.8: from the tribe of Zebulun, twelve thousand, from the tribe of Joseph, twelve thousand, from the tribe of Benjamin, twelve thousand were sealed.
NET.9: After these things I looked, and here was an enormous crowd that no one could count, made up of persons from every nation, tribe, people, and language, standing before the throne and before the Lamb dressed in long white robes, and with palm branches in their hands.
NET.10: They were shouting out in a loud voice, “Salvation belongs to our God, to the one seated on the throne, and to the Lamb!”
NET.11: And all the angels stood there in a circle around the throne and around the elders and the four living creatures, and they threw themselves down with their faces to the ground before the throne and worshiped God,
NET.12: saying, “Amen! Praise and glory, and wisdom and thanksgiving, and honor and power and strength be to our God for ever and ever. Amen!”
NET.13: Then one of the elders asked me, “These dressed in long white robes — who are they and where have they come from?”
NET.14: So I said to him, “My lord, you know the answer.” Then he said to me, “These are the ones who have come out of the great tribulation. They have washed their robes and made them white in the blood of the Lamb!
NET.15: For this reason they are before the throne of God, and they serve him day and night in his temple, and the one seated on the throne will shelter them.
NET.16: They will never go hungry or be thirsty again, and the sun will not beat down on them, nor any burning heat,
NET.17: because the Lamb in the middle of the throne will shepherd them and lead them to springs of living water, and God will wipe away every tear from their eyes.”
